{"step_by_step_thinking": "The patient presented with severe hydrophobia, which is a symptom of rabies. Corneal scrapings were obtained, and we need to identify the best test for diagnosing rabies. The documents mention that RT-PCR can detect rabies virus RNA in various samples, including saliva and skin biopsies. Since corneal scrapings are a type of skin sample, RT-PCR would be a suitable test for detecting rabies virus RNA. Negri bodies are intracytoplasmic inclusion bodies found in neurons and are not typically detected in corneal scrapings. Antibodies to rabies virus are usually detected in serum or CSF, not in corneal scrapings. Indirect immunofluorescence is used for detecting antibodies, not the virus itself.", "answer_choice": "C"}
